text: Code refactoring is the process of improving your code's structure without changing what it does. Think of it like tidying a messy kitchen: you reorganize drawers and clean countertops, but you don't change the recipes. The main goal is to make code easier to read and understand. When developers work on a project for a long time, they often add quick fixes that create "technical debt" — messy, complicated code that becomes hard to modify. Refactoring helps pay off this debt by simplifying confusing parts, removing duplicate code, and giving variables clearer names. While it may take extra time now, it saves much more time later when you need to make changes.
